notes: Public-source v0.1 database for the global AI stack.
This is a strategic dependency graph, not a supplier-contract database.
Edges marked fact_status=inferred are system-level dependency hypotheses.
Global comparison lens: US, EU, China, Middle East, Canada.
Designed for Codex ingestion and website graph generation.

nodes.name: Global AI Stack
nodes.description: The worldwide AI stack: models, cloud, compute, chips, data, distribution, tooling, regulation and opportunity signals.

nodes.name: Frontier Model Layer
nodes.description: Closed and open-weight frontier model providers.

nodes.name: AI Cloud / Model Platform Layer
nodes.description: Cloud platforms hosting models, agents, guardrails, enterprise AI APIs and managed infrastructure.

nodes.name: GPU / Accelerator Compute Layer
nodes.description: High-end GPUs, accelerators, AI superclusters and AI-specific cloud capacity.

nodes.name: Advanced Chip Supply Chain
nodes.description: AI accelerators depend on HBM, foundries, lithography, packaging, networking and export controls.

nodes.name: AI Data Center / Energy Layer
nodes.description: Power, cooling, land, networking and grid connection for AI data centers and AI factories.

nodes.name: Model Distribution / Registry Layer
nodes.description: Model hubs, package registries and distribution paths for open models, datasets and AI artifacts.

nodes.name: AI Serving / Inference Runtime Layer
nodes.description: Software that determines latency, throughput, cost and deployment reliability.

nodes.name: AI Tooling / Observability Layer
nodes.description: Training orchestration, observability, evaluation, experiment tracking, deployment, monitoring and governance.

nodes.name: AI Safety / Governance / Compliance
nodes.description: Regulation, model risk management, security, compliance, evaluation, audit and policy constraints.

nodes.name: Training Data / Data Rights Layer
nodes.description: High-quality data, proprietary datasets, synthetic data, licensing, privacy and data governance.

nodes.name: Enterprise Buyer / Workflow Layer
nodes.description: Enterprises and governments adopting AI inside workflows: productivity, software, legal, finance, manufacturing, defense, healthcare.

nodes.name: US AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: US-based model labs, hyperscalers, chips, AI clouds and enterprise platforms.

nodes.name: China AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: Chinese model companies, cloud platforms, domestic AI accelerators and state/consumer deployment.

nodes.name: European AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: European model companies, AI Factories, sovereign cloud and regulation-driven adoption.

nodes.name: Middle East AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: Gulf AI infrastructure, sovereign funds, AI cloud ambitions and Arabic-language model opportunity.

nodes.name: Canadian AI Ecosystem
nodes.description: Cohere and research/enterprise AI ecosystem.

nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.description: Frontier models, ChatGPT, API platform, enterprise AI, Codex and agentic workflows.

nodes.name: Anthropic / Claude
nodes.description: Claude models, Claude Code, enterprise/government/industry solutions and API platform.

nodes.name: Google DeepMind / Gemini

nodes.description: Gemini models integrated into Google ecosystem and cloud AI platform.

nodes.name: Meta AI / Llama
nodes.strategic_role: Open-weight model and consumer AI platform
nodes.description: Llama model family and Meta AI assistant ecosystem.

nodes.name: xAI / Grok

nodes.description: Grok models, unified API and Colossus/supercluster positioning.

nodes.name: Mistral AI
nodes.description: European model company building frontier models, assistants, agents, services and enterprise AI platform.

nodes.name: Cohere

nodes.name: DeepSeek

nodes.name: Alibaba Cloud / Qwen

nodes.name: Baidu AI / ERNIE ecosystem

nodes.name: Perplexity
nodes.description: Enterprise AI research/search platform over files, web and tools with citation-driven answers and security positioning.

nodes.name: G42 / Core42 ecosystem
nodes.description: AI ecosystem/holding company across infrastructure and vertical AI in UAE.

opportunities.title: Global GPU capacity intelligence and allocation broker
opportunities.reason: The global model and AI cloud stack is bottlenecked by accelerator availability, data-center capacity and power. A broker/observability layer can help startups, enterprises and investors locate capacity and plan cost/risk.

opportunities.title: Model router with sovereignty, latency, cost and compliance policies
opportunities.reason: Enterprises now have many models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Grok, Qwen, Mistral, Cohere and others. A policy-aware router can optimize quality, cost, data residency, latency and regulatory constraints.

opportunities.title: AI supply-chain risk OS for models, data and dependencies
opportunities.reason: Open models, closed APIs, model hubs and enterprise integrations create a new AI supply chain. Buyers need provenance, license, safety, source and dependency visibility.

opportunities.title: Europe-vs-world AI strategic comparison dashboard
opportunities.reason: The global graph reveals Europe’s strengths and weaknesses against the US, China and Middle East: regulation, ASML, Mistral, AI Factories vs compute/chip/cloud exposure. This can become a paid intelligence product.

opportunities.title: AI inference FinOps and observability layer
opportunities.reason: Inference will dominate costs as AI moves from training demos into production. A cross-model observability layer can monitor cost, latency, quality, safety and usage across providers.

opportunities.title: AI data-center energy and grid intelligence
opportunities.reason: Data center power and cooling is becoming a strategic bottleneck. Software that maps grid capacity, cooling options, renewables, permits and AI workload demand can serve cloud, real estate and public-sector buyers.

opportunities.title: Multilingual public-sector AI deployment kit
opportunities.reason: Europe and the Middle East both have multilingual/public-sector needs. The gap is not only models, but deployment, evaluation, audit, retrieval, translation and data controls.

opportunities.title: AI procurement due-diligence engine
opportunities.reason: Every enterprise choosing AI models/cloud is now making dependency, privacy, cost and regulatory bets. A due-diligence engine can score providers and architecture choices against real dependency graph logic.

opportunities.title: Enterprise runtime for open-weight models
opportunities.reason: Open-weight models from Meta/Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen and Mistral create demand for secure deployment, quantization, serving, monitoring and governance in enterprises.

opportunities.title: HBM / memory supply risk intelligence
opportunities.reason: AI accelerators depend on high-bandwidth memory and advanced packaging. Buyers and governments need visibility into memory constraints, supplier concentration and substitution timelines.

opportunities.title: Benchmark and evaluation truth layer for model buyers
opportunities.reason: Model choice is noisy. Enterprises need evals tied to their data, costs, latency, safety, hallucination risk, tool use and compliance—not generic leaderboard theater.

opportunities.title: Cross-cloud AI continuity and fallback layer
opportunities.reason: AI applications increasingly depend on a few model APIs and clouds. A failover layer across models/providers can protect enterprises from outages, pricing shocks, rate limits and geopolitical constraints.
